step_by_step_thinking: Agreeing with agent1’s summary, we see that in noncompetitive inhibition, the availability of active sites on the enzyme reduces, which leads to a lower maximal velocity (\(V_{\text{max}}\)). However, since these inhibitors don't affect how strongly the enzyme binds to its substrate, the \(K_m\) value stays the same. Thus,
